name: Spinach Salad in Fresh Orange Dressing with Pine Nuts
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel the oranges and remove the white pith. Over a bowl positioned to catch the juice, separate the segments of each orange from the membrane with a sharp knife, discarding the seeds. Squeeze the residual juice from the membranes into the bowl and reserve.
2. Combine the spinach and watercress in a mixing bowl.
3. Put the pine nuts in a small sauté pan and cook over medium heat for about 6 minutes, shaking the pan occasionally, until the nuts are golden brown. Transfer the pine nuts to a small bowl.
4. Put the shallots, garlic, vinegars, and reserved orange juice into the sauté pan. Bring to a bowl over low heat. Stir in the freshly ground pepper, then pour the dressing over the spinach and watercress. Toss to coat.
5. Arrange the mixture on salad plates. Spoon orange segments atop each portion and garnish with the pine nuts and, if desired, orange wheels.
MAKING ORANGE WHEELS
- Slice an unpeeled orange into 1/4-inch rounds. Make a cut halfway across each round from the peel to the core, then twist the sides of the cut in opposite directions so that the top half of the round will stand upright when placed on the salad.
